Re: Conflict of Interest Opj-nion Request
Santa Cruz City Councj-lmember
Dear Sir or Madam:
This letter is to supplement my December 12, 1990, letter with
regard to the captioned matter. 
Upon Mr. Rittenhouse's review of
that 
letter, 
he noted three factual 
errors which he wished
corrected.
At page t!'Io, first paragraph, I stated that the Santa Cruz
Hardware Building, oD a parcel of property owned by RBIC, 1S
currently vacant. In fact, that building was destroyed in the
earthquake and., accordingly, the subject parcel is currently bare
l-and and does not contain a vacant building. 
Secondfy, in that
same paragraph, I refer to two other parcels on Pacific Avenue, one
which formerly contained. the Shockley's Jewelry Store Build.ing and
the other which formerly contained the LiIy Marlane's Restaurant
Bui-lding. fn fact, this was one parcel which formerly contained
the two buildings. 
The third and final correction should be made
with reference to the second paragraph on page two. There I state
that, upon the death of Mr. Rittenhouse's aunt, ME. Rittenhouse's
father would be entitled to receive the trust assets. In fact,
Mr. Rittenhouse's father would be entitled. to receive fifty percent
of the trust assets while Mr. Rittenhouse's two cousins, the two
children of Mr. Rittenhouse's deceased uncle who was named. as a
remalnderman in the trust along with Mr. Rittenhouse's father, are
each entitled to receive twenty-five percent of the trust assets.
The fact remains that, unless Mr. Rittenhouse's father predeeeases
hj-m, there is no guarantee that Mr. Rittenhouse will ever receive
any of these trust assets.
Thank you for your attention to the

Fair Political Practices Commission

428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814
Re: Conflict of Interest Opinion Request - Santa Cruz Ci-ty
Councilmember
Dear Sir or Madan:
As you know, oo october !7, 1990, the city of santa ctuz
experienced. a devastatj-ng earthquake. As a result of that
di-saster, thirty two buildlngs on a five block section of Pacific
Avenue in the City," downtown district 
were severely damaged or
d.estroyed. At thi; time, the City Council is regularly receiving
permit requests from the affectecl property owners which, if
lranted, 
would a11ow the 
property owners to 
commence
reconstruction.
After the earthquake the City expanded its 
Redevelopment
Survey Area to include the entire downtown d.istrict including
pacific Avenue. City Redevelopment Department staff estimate that
approximately fifteen percent of the land' within the City is now
plit of the Redevelopment Survey Area. Total- assessed value of
iroperty within the Clty approximates two billion dollars of which
SZS-Cl, OO0,0OO (roughly i/3 ) is within the Redevelopment Survey Area.
The City Council serves as the Clty's Redevelopment Agency.
On November 6, 1990, ME. Louis Rittenhouse was elected to
serve on the santa cruz city council. 
Mr. Rittenhouse has a
variety of interests in a number of downtown parcels on Pacific
A-,,enue. Sonne of these parcels containcC build:ngs r';l:lcl: I"'ere
destroyed in the earthcluake. Other parcels are located within 300
feet o1 parcels which contained buildings which were destroyed..
Other paicels are between 3OO and. 2500 feet from parcels which
contained bulld.ings which were destroyed'. Finally, other parcels
are over 2500 teet from parcels which contained buildings which
were destroyed.
The purpose of this letter is to describe Mr. Rittenhouse's
ownership lnterests in the Rittenhouse parcels on Pacific Avenue
and, in light of those various interests, to ask for advice from
the Commissi-on as to the circumstances under which Mr. Rittenhouse
can properly vote on a variety of issues which will come to the
City Council- pertaining to downtown reconstruction.


Fair. Politicaf Prac
t2/12/90
Paqe 2
Comm .
Mr. Rittenhouse is interested 
in various Paci.fic Avenue
parcels as a result of his reLationship with a corporation and as
a re6uIt of his interest in two trusts,
interests wiII be discussed separately,
Each of these three
First Mr. Rittenhouse
owns stock 1n the Rj-ttenhouse Buj-lding Investments Corporation
(RBIC), a closely helcl farnj,ly corporation. Mr, Rittenhouse owns
ten of the corporation's 2000 shares (i.e. 0.5%). In additlon Mr.
Rj-ttenhouse is an employee of RBIC and he derives approximately 50t
of his income (over $10,000.00) from RBIC. On Pacific Avenue, RBIC
owns the Elatiron Buildj-ng (current.Ly vacant). It also oeTns the
Santa cruz Hardware Buj-lding (currently vacant). Both of these
buiJ.dings are within 300 feet of the parcel which formally
containeil the largest builcling destroyed as a resuft of the
earthquake, the St. George Hotef. O$7ners of the St. George Hotel
parcel are currently in the process of submitting plans to the City
for a replacement project to be built on that parcel. In addition,
the RBIC owns t\tro other parcels on Pacj-fic Avenue. One parce.L
formerly contained. the Shockley's Jewelry Sto.r.e Building. 
The
other formerly contained the Lily Marlane/s Restaurant Builaling.
Both of these buildings were demolished as a result of the
earthquake and currently each of these parcel-s 1s bare land. Both
of these parcels are within 2500 feet of the St. George Hotel
parcel as well as other parcels of property along Pacific Avenue
which had buildings which were destroyed as a result of the
earthquake. Both parcels will be the subject of development
proposals j-n the foreseeable future.
Second.Iy, Mr. Rittenhouse is the contingent remainderman to a
trust lrhich was estabfished by his grandmother ( no\., deceased). Mr.
Rittenhouse's father is the trustee. Mr. Rittenhouse's aunt is the
tru6t beneficiary who is entitfeai to receive income from the trust
for her lifetime. 
Upon the death of Mr. Rj.ttenhousers aunt, Mr.
Rittenhouse's father eroulal be entitled to receive the trust assets.
If his father di-es before his aunt di-es then Mr. Rittenhouse would
diviCe his father's share of the trust assets 1'7ith his sister.
Because both his aunt and his father are aIive, there is no
guarantee at this time that Mr. Rittenhouse will ever receive any
of the trust's assets. The trust corpus is comprj-sed of stocks anal
bonds and a parcel of property on Pacific Avenue which is now bare
as a result of the earthquake. That parcel, like the parcels ovrned
by RBIC, is within 300 feet of some parcels along Pacific Avenue
upon $rhich construction will eventually take place, within 2500
feet of other such parcels, and over 2500 feet from other such
parcels. The parcel will be the subject of a development proposal
j-n the foreseeab.Le future. 
As trustee, Mr. Rittenhouse's father
has the discretion to retain this parcel of property or to sell it.
Accordingly it i.s possible that the parcel may not be part of the
trust corpus when and if trust assets are eventually recej-ved by
Mr. Rittenhouse.


Fair PoIitical Prac. comm.
12/12/90
Page 3
Finally, 
Mr. Rittenhouse is a remainderman under another
This trust 
lras established by Mr. Rittenhouse's
granitfather. The trustee is Mr. Rittenhouse's stepmother (who is
also secretary of RBIC). This trust corpus is comprised of two
aaaets, It has a singl,e parcel of property on Pacific Avenue which
j-s now bare as a result of the earthquake and which \,ri11 be the
subject of a development proposal in the foreseeable future, 
In
aaldition it has 50? of the stock (i.e. 1000 shares) of RBIC. The
trustee votes thi6 stock. Trust income j.s to be divided equally
among Mr. Rittenhouse's father, aunt and uncle (now deceased).
Upon the death of all three trust beneficiaries, the trust property
is to be d.ivided equa]-]-y among Mr. Rittenhouse, hj-s sister and two
cousins. Once again the trustee has the discretj-on to retain the
Pacific Avenue parcel ox to sell it. 
Accordingly, as with the
other trust, 
at this time thelre is no guarantee that 
Mr.
Rittenhouse will ever take title to the Pacifie Avenue parcel owned
by the trust.
Given the facts set forth in thj,s fetter, on behalf of Mr.
Rittenhouse, we would appreciate the Commj,ssion's opini-on with
regard to the following:
1. 
Under what circumstances may Mr, Rittenhouse, as a
Councj-Inember or Redevelopment Agency member, participate 
in
Counc j-I decisions retative to the development of parcels on Pacific
Avenue which are not ownecl by RBIC or either of the two Rittenhouse
trusts?
2. Are there any circumstances under which Mr, Rittenhouse,
as a Councilmember or Redevelopment Agency member, may participate
in Council or Agency declsions relative to the deveJ-opment of
parcels on Pacific Avenue o$ined by RBIC?
3. Are there any cj-rcumstances uncler which Mr. Rittenhouse,
as a Councj-lmember or Redevelopment Agency member, may participate
in Council or Agency clecisions rel-ative to the clevelopment of
parcels on Pacific Avenue o$rned by ej-ther of the two Rittenhouse
trusts?
4. As a result of the earthquake a significant percentage of
the Pacific Avenue infrastructure, surface street and. other public
inprovernents wiIl have to be redesigned anal replaced. 
These
improvements vii11 affect and. service all parcels along the subject
portion of Pacific Avenue. Unaler what circumstances may l,lr.
Rittenhouse, as a Councilmember or 
Redevelopment Agency menber,
participate in decisions pertaining to the redesign and replacement
of public j,mprovements a]-ong Pacific Avenue?


Fair Political 
Prac . comm.
72/72/90
Page 4
5. 
To what extent, if 
any, is Mr. Rittenhouse as a
Redevelopment Agency menber, precludecl fron participatj,ng in
decisions pertaining to development within the Redevelopment Survey
Area or to the appropriation of Redevelopment Agency Sunds?
6. 
If the Mayor appointed Mr. Rj,ttenhouse to a three
Councilmenber "downto\tn subcommittee" assigned to focus on the
reconstruction of downtown santa cruz, would Mr. Rittenhouse's
partj-cipation on the subconmittee be subject to the 
same
lj.mitations outlined i,n your responses to questions 1 through 5?
we would appreciate the Conmission's prompt response to the
foregoing opinj-on request lnasmuch as the City council 1s current.Iy
being presented with issues which caIL all _ of the foregoing
questions into p1ay. Thank you very much f otltloux - assistance.
